_meta.what: Operational freshwater cyanoHAB monitoring/forecasting TOOLS a user can access today. Each attribute traces to a source dossier key in ../../Research/_sources/ (resolves in ../../Research/REFERENCES.md). 'n/r' = not reported by the sources (never guessed).
_meta.scope: Freshwater cyanobacteria only. Where a parent program also covers marine HABs, the row is scoped to its freshwater component and flagged (marine_parent).

quality_tiers.established-operational: Deployed public product with peer-reviewed AND out-of-sample validation + a baseline.
quality_tiers.operational-unbenchmarked: Deployed/public but no skill metric stated in the corpus.
quality_tiers.vendor-self-report: Commercial; validation claimed but no disclosed baseline or peer review.
quality_tiers.research-grade: Peer-reviewed model with reported skill; not an operational public product.

_meta.fields_note: coverage = country/region/state served; spatial_res_m = spatial resolution in metres (or 'point (in-situ)'/'n/r'). Added 2026-07-01 for the forecast-tools table.
_meta.method_vs_data_note: model_type is the METHOD (how it turns data into output). 'Data fusion' is NOT a method — it is a DATA STRATEGY (combining multiple sources), recorded in data_strategy, and is carried out BY a statistical/ML or mechanistic method. (The models slide keeps algorithm and data-signal as separate axes; this mirrors that.)

tools.name: EPA CyAN / CyANWeb
tools.quality_basis: Operational; Cyanobacteria Index validated 84% accuracy / 87% precision / 90% recall vs combined toxin+cell ground truth [ACAD-045]; 60%/69% true-positive vs state events/advisories [ACAD-006]
tools.note: The spectral backbone of US freshwater HAB monitoring. Detects biomass presence, NOT toxin identity/level; blind to sub-pixel small lakes and nearshore scums.

tools.name: EPA experimental 7-day cyanoHAB forecast
tools.quality_basis: Underlying model peer-reviewed & tested on a fully out-of-sample year (2021): 90% accuracy, AUC 0.95, but only 49% precision (overpredicts — health-protective bias) [ACAD-050]
tools.note: SAME SYSTEM as research model M1 (Slide 2): the operational product IS the Bayesian/INLA fusion model. Counted once here as a product, once there as a model.
tools.data_strategy: multi-source fusion (satellite CI + weather + lake morphology)

tools.name: NOAA Lake Erie HAB Forecast + bulletin
tools.horizon: nowcast + ≥ 96-hr (~4-day) bloom-position forecast + seasonal severity outlook
tools.quality_basis: Long-running operational product; no accuracy/skill figure stated anywhere in the corpus (n/r). Underlying transport models compared vs satellite bloom fields [ACAD-024]
tools.note: Couples a hydrodynamic model with satellite imagery + particle transport. Product side of research model M9.

tools.name: NOAA HAB Monitoring System
tools.quality_basis: Operational monitoring/short-term products; no skill metric in corpus
tools.note: Parent program covers 13 water bodies incl. coastal/estuarine; scoped here to its freshwater component (marine excluded per scope).

tools.name: Florida DEP Algal Bloom Dashboard
tools.quality_basis: Sampling + citizen-report dashboard; not a predictive model (no skill metric)
tools.note: Parent dashboard prominently covers marine red tide (Karenia brevis); scoped here to its freshwater cyanoHAB component.

tools.name: NY NYHABS notifications & map
tools.org: NY State Dept. of Environmental Conservation
tools.note: Crowdsourced 'Know it, Avoid it, Report it' model — a design reference for an accessible field tool.

tools.name: California SWAMP FHAB + satellite screening
tools.org: California State Water Resources Control Board
tools.quality_basis: Statewide program pairing satellite remote screening for larger waterbodies with ambient monitoring; no skill metric in corpus
tools.note: One of the few state programs explicitly fusing a remote-sensing screen with in-situ monitoring.

tools.name: Ohio HAB response (Lake Erie region)
tools.org: Ohio EPA / Ohio Dept. of Health
tools.quality_basis: Mature state advisory program implementing federal cyanotoxin thresholds; satellite-imagery-supported; not predictive
tools.note: Among the most mature state HAB advisory frameworks; directly relevant to Lake Erie.

tools.name: Utah / Oregon recreational advisory systems
tools.quality_basis: Toxin-confirmed tiered advisories; explicitly note only a fraction of waterbodies are monitored [SLG-008]
tools.note: Candid coverage-gap statements ('when in doubt, stay out') illustrate where satellite screening could complement sparse in-situ monitoring.

tools.name: BlueGreen BGi Water Health Intelligence
tools.quality_basis: Commercial platform fusing satellite + in-situ + weather (5+ yr); no disclosed validation or baseline
tools.note: Closest commercial analog to a satellite+in-situ fusion tool; claims unverified in corpus.
tools.method_note: Vendor analytics platform; underlying algorithm undisclosed — classified statistical/ML as the best-supported assumption. Its multi-source FUSION is a data strategy, recorded in data_strategy (not a method).

tools.name: CyanoLakes
tools.horizon: nowcast + forecast (vendor-stated up to 3 weeks; '80%/70% at 1-/2-week lead')
tools.quality_basis: Commercial satellite subscription; accuracy figures are vendor self-report with no disclosed baseline
tools.note: Private-sector analog to the CyAN signal, with a paid forecast claim.

tools.name: EOMAP water-quality service
tools.quality_basis: Physics-based commercial retrieval (chlorophyll/CDOM/turbidity); no disclosed HAB-specific validation in corpus
tools.note: Physics-based (non-ML) commercial alternative for water-quality retrieval.
